summary: Key escalation events: April 2025 threats then walkback, July 2025 drafted firing letter then said 'highly unlikely', Dec 29 2025 said 'love to fire him' and 'maybe I still might', plans to announce Fed chair nominee in January 2026
content:
Timeline of events:
- Feb 2025: Powell says firing Fed chair "not permitted under the law"
- April 17, 2025: Trump posts "Powell's termination cannot come fast enough"
- April 22, 2025: Trump denies plans to fire Powell, de-escalation
- July 15-16, 2025: Trump drafts letter firing Powell, shows to House Republicans, polls them. Next day says "highly unlikely" to fire
- Dec 11, 2025: Trump interviews Kevin Warsh for Fed chair; Fed pencils in just one rate cut for 2026
- Dec 12, 2025: Fed "Trump-proofs" itself by early reappointment of regional bank presidents
- Dec 29, 2025: Trump says he'd "love to fire" Powell, "maybe I still might", considering "gross incompetence" lawsuit. Says he'll announce nominee "in January sometime". Frontrunner: Kevin Hassett. Others: Warsh, Waller, Bowman, Rieder.

CP as of Jan 3, 2026: exactly 9.00%
209 forecasters on the question
Resolution window for underlying: before May 1, 2026
